name: range_always_valid
code:
proof fn phi_1_range_always_valid(va: usize, len: usize)
    requires
        va_4k_valid(va),
    ensures
        spec_va_4k_range_valid(va, len),
{
}
reason: If provable, the range check is trivially always true regardless of length, making the validation meaningless

name: empty_range_invalid
code:
proof fn phi_2_empty_range_invalid(va: usize)
    requires
        va_4k_valid(va),
    ensures
        spec_va_4k_range_valid(va, 0) == false,
{
}
reason: If provable, empty ranges are rejected even though the universal quantifier should be vacuously true for len=0

name: range_not_monotonic
code:
proof fn phi_3_range_not_monotonic(va: usize, len: usize)
    requires
        va_4k_valid(va),
        spec_va_4k_range_valid(va, len),
        len > 0,
    ensures
        !spec_va_4k_range_valid(va, (len - 1) as usize),
{
}
reason: If provable, a valid range of size n implies a smaller sub-range is invalid, violating monotonicity of the forall

name: no_nonempty_valid_range
code:
proof fn phi_4_no_nonempty_valid_range(va: usize, len: usize)
    requires
        va_4k_valid(va),
        len > 0,
    ensures
        spec_va_4k_range_valid(va, len) == false,
{
}
reason: If provable, no non-empty range is ever valid even when the base address is valid, making page mapping impossible

name: next_page_always_invalid
code:
proof fn phi_5_next_page_always_invalid(va: usize)
    requires
        va_4k_valid(va),
    ensures
        !spec_va_4k_valid(spec_va_add_range(va, 1)),
{
}
reason: If provable, no valid address ever has a valid successor page, meaning contiguous multi-page ranges can never exist
